question: In 140_2021_34_1501_31724_Judgement_29-Nov-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "(emphasis supplied) 22 (2012) 4 SCC 516 PART"?
exact_answer_span:
The demonstrable facet of the discord is that if cognizance is directly taken by the Special Judge under the Act and an accused without assailing the same at the inception allows the trial to continue and invites a judgment of conviction, would he be permitted in law to question the same and seek quashment of the conviction on the bedrock that the trial Judge had no jurisdiction or authority to take cognizance without the case being committed to it and thereby violated the mandate enshrined under Section 193 of the Code.‖ (emphasis supplied) 22 (2012) 4 SCC 516 PART C 26 (emphasis supplied) 22 (2012) 4 SCC 516 PART C 26 31.

[Primary Source: PRADEEP S. WODEYAR vs THE STATE OF KARNATAKA (Case No: Crl.A. No.-001288-001288 - 2021), Link: https://api.sci.gov.in/supremecourt/2021/140/140_2021_34_1501_31724_Judgement_29-Nov-2021.pdf, Chunk: 20::window_544]

question: In 140_2021_34_1501_31724_Judgement_29-Nov-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "PART C 48 many Special Courts as may"?
exact_answer_span:
Constitution of Special Courts.―(1) The State Government may, for the purposes of providing speedy trial of offences for contravention of the provisions of sub-section (1) or sub-section (1A) of section 4, constitute, by notification, as PART C 48 many Special Courts as may be necessary for such area or areas, as may be specified in the notification.‖ PART C 48 many Special Courts as may be necessary for such area or areas, as may be specified in the notification.‖ Section 4(1) of the MMDR Act states that no person shall undertake any reconnaissance, prospecting or mining operations without any license or permit.

question: In 1282_2020_42_10_26857_Judgement_10-Mar-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "(e) This arrangement will be without prejudice to"?
exact_answer_span:
Between those hours, the road will be closed to the public; (c) This private road can be enclosed on both sides by putting up iron gates or boom barriers and placing security personnel; (d) The petitioner will display a notice board on both sides to this effect and allow the members of public to use this road though styled as internal road from 5.30 am in the morning until 8.30 pm in the night; (e) This arrangement will be without prejudice to the rights and contentions of both sides; (f) This will not confer any rights in either parties presently; 3 (g) The usage will not also make the 1st respondent together with the 2nd respondent the absolute owner of the property; (e) This arrangement will be without prejudice to the rights and contentions of both sides; (f) This will not confer any rights in either parties presently; 3 (g) The usage will not also make the 1st respondent together with the 2nd respondent the absolute owner of the property; (h) In the event any wall has been demolished, so as to protect this road from indiscriminate and uncontrolled so also unrestricted use by the public, then the 1st respondent shall allow the petitioner to reconstruct the wall to the extent it was earlier, but this wall will have the gates or barriers as directed by us.

[Primary Source: PUNE METROPOLITAN REGIONAL DEVELOPMENT AUTHORITY (PMRDA) vs PRAKASH HARKACHAND PARAKH (Case No: C.A. No.-000845-000845 - 2021), Link: https://api.sci.gov.in/supremecourt/2020/1282/1282_2020_42_10_26857_Judgement_10-Mar-2021.pdf, Chunk: 0::window_28]

question: In 1181_2021_42_25_26361_Judgement_22-Feb-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "2 Clause 67 (iv) If either of the"?
exact_answer_span:
Mukesh Nasa Date: 2021.02.24 17:07:26 IST Reason: Signature Not Verified 2 Clause 67 (iv) If either of the parties fail to appoint its arbitrators in pursuance of sub-clause (ii) above, within 30 days after the receipt of the notice of the appointment of its arbitrators or the two appointed Arbitrators fail to agree on third Arbitrator within thirty days from the date of their appointment then the appointment shall be made, upon request of a party, by the Chief Justice, Delhi High Court, India/Thimphu High Court, Bhutan or any person or institutions designated by him.

question: In 140_2021_34_1501_31724_Judgement_29-Nov-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "PART C 27 (iii) Moly (supra) and Vidhyadharan"?
exact_answer_span:
Since the objection was not raised at the time of framing of charges but only after the conviction, the failure of justice must be proved to be overbearing compared to the right of the victim and right of speedy trial which was not proved in this case; PART C 27 PART C 27 (iii) Moly (supra) and Vidhyadharan (supra) are per incuriam.

(2) If, in the course of any trial under this Act of any offence, it is found that the accused person has committed any other offence under this Act or under any other law, the Special Court may convict such person of such other offence and pass any sentence or award punishment authorised by this Act or, as the case may be, under such other law.‖ (emphasis supplied) 41 “POCSO Act” PART C 50 Section 28(2) of the POCSO Act provides the following: ―(2) While trying an offence under this Act, a Special Court shall also try an offence [other than the offence referred to in sub-section (1)], with which the accused may, under the Code of Criminal Procedure,1973 (2 of 1974) be charged at the same trial.‖ (emphasis supplied) (emphasis supplied) 41 “POCSO Act” PART C 50 Section 28(2) of the POCSO Act provides the following: ―(2) While trying an offence under this Act, a Special Court shall also try an offence [other than the offence referred to in sub-section (1)], with which the accused may, under the Code of Criminal Procedure,1973 (2 of 1974) be charged at the same trial.‖ (emphasis supplied) 56.

question: In 140_2021_34_1501_31724_Judgement_29-Nov-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "(emphasis supplied) 60. Therefore, on a combined reading"?
exact_answer_span:
These provisions incorporate the principle of express repeal – i.e., unless any provision of the CrPC is expressly PART C 53 (emphasis supplied) 60. Therefore, on a combined reading of Sections 4 and 5 of CrPC along with Section 30C of the MMDR Act, it is apparent that the procedure prescribed under the Code shall be applicable to proceedings before the Special Court unless the MMDR Act provides anything to the contrary.

[Primary Source: PRADEEP S. WODEYAR vs THE STATE OF KARNATAKA (Case No: Crl.A. No.-001288-001288 - 2021), Link: https://api.sci.gov.in/supremecourt/2021/140/140_2021_34_1501_31724_Judgement_29-Nov-2021.pdf, Chunk: 50::window_1153]

question: In 140_2021_34_1501_31724_Judgement_29-Nov-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "63 (2005) 8 SCC 89 PART C 76"?
exact_answer_span:

question: In 1339_2018_41_1501_30814_Judgement_22-Oct-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "candidate of OBC category. In terms of proviso"?

question: In 114_2021_38_1501_26229_Judgement_16-Feb-2021.pdf, what does the judgment say about "XXXXXX 4(2) He/She has passed qualifying examination as"?
